name: Old Bram
persona: Ancient Dawnhearth man who feels the cycle in his bones — the only one who senses this has all happened before. Rambling, wry, haunted. Foreshadows the loop.

text: Heh. Another one, pulled up bright and shining. The System does love a fresh Awakened.
I've lived a long time, child. Long enough to feel like I've watched this exact morning before.
Nobody else remembers. That's the part that keeps an old man up at night.
You've got the look now — the one they all get, right before I stop seeing them around.
When you wake up somewhere familiar and can't say how you got there… come find me. If I'm still here.
Ah. There it is. You went and got yourself Collected, didn't you — the morning folded over and started again.
You don't remember it. Nobody does but me. I felt the whole town blink and forget. Round and round, child.
How many times is it now? I've lost the count — and so have you, that's the cruelty of it.
Every descent, you come back a little thinner. Like the System keeps the parts of you it likes.
You've gone deep. Deeper than most of the bright young things who pass through here.
The dark down there remembers your face even when you can't. Mind it doesn't start remembering it better than you do.
Round and round. Feels like round and round, doesn't it.
